pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly inserts the protocol with this name into the database.
The task description strictly requires splitting the text into these exactly named four steps.
setup_task.sh injects a TEXT_CONTENT string containing these exact keywords which correspond to the respective steps.
setup_task.sh TEXT_CONTENT contains this substring, and the task description explicitly instructs to bold the sentence starting with 'WARNING:'.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically injected protocol data. The target protocol name 'Bradford Protein Assay (Draft)' and its initial single-step text are explicitly injected via setup_task.sh. The expected four split step names ('Reagent Preparation', 'Sample Preparation', 'Assay Execution', 'Measurement and Analysis'), their unique text identifiers ('2000 ug/mL', '1:10', '96-well microplate', '595 nm'), and the required bolded safety warning ('WARNING: Coomassie dye') perfectly match the initial setup script payload and task instructions.
